title: A Mind for Numbers
author: Barbara Oakley
subline: How to excel at math and science (even if you flunked algebra).
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A brain-network diagram: 18 nodes connected in a graph, illustrating 'focused' (dense) and 'diffuse' (sparse) modes. The right half is the diffuse mode — sparser, more spread out. ONE node is RED. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1280)" fill="none">
    <!-- Faint enclosing ellipse -->
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="0" rx="640" ry="280" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4"/>
    <!-- 18 nodes (9 left = focused, 9 right = diffuse) -->
    <g fill="#cccccc" stroke="#cccccc">
      <!-- Left side (focused, dense) -->
      <line x1="-460" y1="-100" x2="-360" y2="-40"/>
      <line x1="-460" y1="-100" x2="-400" y2="40"/>
      <line x1="-360" y1="-40" x2="-400" y2="40"/>
      <line x1="-360" y1="-40" x2="-280" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="-400" y1="40" x2="-280" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="-280" y1="-100" x2="-200" y2="-40"/>
      <line x1="-200" y1="-40" x2="-200" y2="60"/>
      <line x1="-200" y1="-40" x2="-100" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="-200" y1="60" x2="-100" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="-200" y1="60" x2="-100" y2="20"/>
      <line x1="-100" y1="-100" x2="-100" y2="20"/>
      <!-- Right side (diffuse, sparse) -->
      <line x1="100" y1="-100" x2="200" y2="40"/>
      <line x1="200" y1="40" x2="320" y2="-80"/>
      <line x1="320" y1="-80" x2="440" y2="20"/>
      <line x1="200" y1="40" x2="440" y2="20"/>
      <line x1="100" y1="-100" x2="320" y2="-80"/>
      <line x1="440" y1="20" x2="560" y2="-60"/>
    </g>
    <g fill="#cccccc" stroke="none">
      <circle cx="-460" cy="-100" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="-360" cy="-40" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="-400" cy="40" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="-280" cy="-100" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="-200" cy="-40" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="-200" cy="60" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="-100" cy="-100" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="-100" cy="20" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="100" cy="-100" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="200" cy="40" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="320" cy="-80" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="440" cy="20" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="560" cy="-60" r="6"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The 'eureka' node — RED, larger, with a faint red ring -->
    <circle cx="560" cy="-60" r="16" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <circle cx="560" cy="-60" r="28" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1.5" opacity="0.5"/>
    <!-- Labels -->
    <text x="-280" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.2em">FOCUSED</text>
    <text x="320" y="160" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.2em">DIFFUSE</text>
    <!-- A separator (the 'switch') -->
    <line x1="0" y1="-160" x2="0" y2="120" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 4"/>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: Oakley's central frame: learning happens in two modes — focused (left half, dense graph, tightly connected) and diffuse (right half, sparse graph, more spread out). The 'eureka' moment happens in the diffuse mode. The rightmost node is RED and 3x size, the moment of insight. Title sits at the top, weight 500 command, mixed-case. Brand 'FOCUSED / DIFFUSE' in the bottom-right; pill 'z² = 1' at the bottom — the simplest possible insight that requires both modes to discover.
